annotations.label: Solvable group (lead)

anchor.snippet: a solvable group or soluble group is a group that can be constructed from abelian groups using extensions

annotations.note: `IsSolvable G` is defined as the existence of `n` with `derivedSeries G n = ⊥`, equivalent to the extension-of-abelian-groups characterization.

annotations.label: Solvability of polynomial via Galois group

anchor.snippet: a polynomial equation is solvable in radicals if and only if the corresponding Galois group is solvable

annotations.note: Only one direction of Abel–Ruffini is formalized (solvable-by-radicals root ⇒ solvable Galois group); the converse is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solvable Galois group from smallest field extension

anchor.snippet: The smallest Galois field extension

annotations.note: No matching specific worked example was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solvable group (subnormal series definition)

anchor.snippet: A group G is called solvable if it has a subnormal series whose factor groups (quotient groups) are all abelian

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `IsSolvable` via the derived series; the equivalent characterization in terms of a subnormal series with abelian quotients is not stated.

annotations.label: Derived series definition of solvable

anchor.snippet: if its derived series , the descending normal series

annotations.note: `derivedSeries G n` and `isSolvable_def : IsSolvable G ↔ ∃ n, derivedSeries G n = ⊥` provide exactly this definition.

annotations.label: Derived length

anchor.snippet: The least n such that G ( n ) = 1 is called the derived length of the solvable group G

annotations.note: Mathlib has `LieAlgebra.derivedLength` for Lie algebras but no derived-length notion for groups.

annotations.label: Finite solvable via composition series

anchor.snippet: For finite groups, an equivalent definition is that a solvable group is a group with a composition series all of whose factors are cyclic groups of prime order

annotations.note: The equivalence between solvability of finite groups and composition series with prime-cyclic factors is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Abelian groups are solvable

anchor.snippet: The basic example of solvable groups are abelian groups

annotations.note: Provided as a low-priority instance `CommGroup.isSolvable : IsSolvable G` for `CommGroup G`.

annotations.label: Nilpotent groups are solvable

anchor.snippet: all nilpotent groups are solvable

annotations.note: `IsNilpotent.to_isSolvable` is an instance that derives `IsSolvable G` from `IsNilpotent G`.

annotations.label: Quaternion group is solvable

anchor.snippet: the quaternion group is a solvable group given by the group extension

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `QuaternionGroup` but no specific `IsSolvable (QuaternionGroup n)` lemma was found.

annotations.label: Extensions of solvable groups are solvable

anchor.snippet: if

annotations.note: `solvable_of_ker_le_range` produces solvability from solvable kernel/image data, encoding the extension property.

annotations.label: S_3 solvable non-nilpotent

anchor.snippet: A small example of a solvable, non-nilpotent group is the symmetric group S 3

annotations.note: No specific lemma stating `IsSolvable (Equiv.Perm (Fin 3))` and its non-nilpotence was located.

annotations.label: Every group of order < 60 is solvable

anchor.snippet: every group with order less than 60 is solvable

annotations.note: No statement about all finite groups of order less than 60 being solvable was found.

annotations.label: Feit–Thompson theorem

anchor.snippet: The Feit–Thompson theorem states that every finite group of odd order is solvable

annotations.note: The Feit–Thompson odd-order theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: S_5 is not solvable

anchor.snippet: The group S 5 is not solvable

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.fin_5_not_solvable : ¬ IsSolvable (Equiv.Perm (Fin 5))` is proved by an explicit commutator computation.

annotations.label: S_n not solvable for n>4

anchor.snippet: we see that S n is not solvable for n > 4

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm.not_solvable` shows `¬ IsSolvable (Equiv.Perm X)` whenever `5 ≤ #X`.

annotations.label: Subgroup quotient of GL_2

anchor.snippet: Consider the subgroups

annotations.note: No matching example computing solvability from this specific GL₂ subgroup chain was found.

annotations.label: Semidirect decomposition remark

anchor.snippet: Notice that this description gives the decomposition

annotations.note: This narrative remark about a semidirect-product decomposition has no corresponding Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Borel subgroup

anchor.snippet: a Borel subgroup is defined as a subgroup which is closed, connected, and solvable

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define Borel subgroups of algebraic groups.

annotations.label: Borel subgroup in GL_3

anchor.snippet: there are the subgroups

annotations.note: No Borel-subgroup construction for GL₃ exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Borel subgroup in product

anchor.section: Borel subgroup in product of simple linear algebraic groups
anchor.snippet: the Borel subgroup can be represented by matrices of the form

annotations.note: Borel subgroups of products of simple algebraic groups are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Z-group

anchor.snippet: Any finite group whose p -Sylow subgroups are cyclic is a semidirect product of two cyclic groups, in particular solvable

annotations.note: `IsZGroup G` is defined as `∀ p prime, ∀ P : Sylow p G, IsCyclic P`; an instance shows `Finite G → IsZGroup G → IsSolvable G`.

annotations.label: Subgroup of solvable is solvable

anchor.snippet: If G is solvable, and H is a subgroup of G , then H is solvable

annotations.note: Instance `subgroup_solvable_of_solvable : IsSolvable G → IsSolvable H` for any subgroup `H`.

annotations.label: Homomorphic image of solvable is solvable

anchor.snippet: If G is solvable, and there is a homomorphism from G onto H , then H is solvable

annotations.note: `solvable_of_surjective` transfers solvability along any surjective group hom.

annotations.label: Three for the price of two

anchor.snippet: G is solvable if and only if both N and G / N are solvable

annotations.note: Both directions are derivable from `solvable_of_ker_le_range`, `subgroup_solvable_of_solvable`, and `solvable_quotient_of_solvable`, but no packaged iff lemma was found.

annotations.label: Direct product of solvable is solvable

anchor.snippet: if G and H are solvable, the direct product G × H is solvable

annotations.note: Instance `solvable_prod : IsSolvable G → IsSolvable G' → IsSolvable (G × G')`.

annotations.label: Solvability closed under extension

anchor.snippet: Solvability is closed under group extension

annotations.note: `solvable_of_ker_le_range` is exactly the closure-under-extensions statement.

annotations.label: Solvability closed under wreath product

anchor.snippet: It is also closed under wreath product

annotations.note: Wreath products of groups and their solvability are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solvable groups of bounded derived length form a variety

anchor.snippet: the solvable groups of derived length at most N form a subvariety of the variety of groups

annotations.note: Varieties of groups and the derived-length subvariety are not formalized.

annotations.label: Burnside's theorem

anchor.snippet: Burnside's theorem states that if G is a finite group of order

annotations.note: Burnside's p^a q^b theorem is not formalized in Mathlib (only the unrelated normal p-complement theorem and Burnside's counting lemma are present).

annotations.label: Supersolvable group

anchor.snippet: a group G is called supersolvable (or supersoluble ) if it has an invariant normal series whose factors are all cyclic

annotations.note: No `IsSupersolvable`/supersolvable predicate exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Supersolvable abelian iff finitely generated

anchor.snippet: an abelian group is supersolvable if and only if it is finitely generated

annotations.note: Depends on a supersolvable definition that is not present.

annotations.label: A_4 solvable but not supersolvable

anchor.snippet: The alternating group A 4 is an example of a finite solvable group that is not supersolvable

annotations.note: No statement about A₄'s solvability vs. supersolvability appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Virtually solvable group

anchor.snippet: A group G is called virtually solvable if it has a solvable subgroup of finite index

annotations.note: Mathlib has no virtually-solvable predicate.

annotations.label: Hypoabelian group

anchor.snippet: A group whose transfinite derived series reaches the trivial group is called a hypoabelian group

annotations.note: Transfinite derived series and hypoabelian groups are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every ordinal is a derived length

anchor.snippet: every ordinal is the derived length of some group

annotations.note: No group-theoretic derived-length notion or this realisation result appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-solvable group

anchor.snippet: A finite group is p-solvable for some prime p if every factor in the composition series is a p-group or has order prime to p

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `IsPSolvable` predicate.

annotations.label: Solvable iff p-solvable for every p

anchor.snippet: A finite group is solvable iff it is p-solvable for every p

annotations.note: Depends on a p-solvable predicate that is not defined in Mathlib.
